introduction: This spec defines the authentication, authorization, and complete REST API surface for af-hub, including OAuth provider integration, auth middleware, RBAC enforcement, and all HTTP handlers for user, workspace, membership, and API key management. Note: requirements referencing Echo middleware and external OAuth HTTP exchange are based on PRD descriptions and should be cross-checked against installed library versions before implementation.

glossary.DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id: Authenticated endpoint to permanently revoke an API key.
glossary.DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id: Admin endpoint to permanently delete an archived workspace with cascade.
glossary.GET /api/v1/auth/providers: Endpoint that lists configured OAuth providers without exposing secrets.
glossary.GET /api/v1/keys: Authenticated endpoint to list API keys; scope depends on authentication method.
glossary.GET /api/v1/users: Admin endpoint to list all user records.
glossary.GET /api/v1/users/:id: Admin endpoint to retrieve a user by ID including memberships and roles.
glossary.GET /api/v1/workspaces: Admin endpoint to list workspaces, optionally including archived ones.
glossary.GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members: Admin endpoint to list workspace members.
glossary.POST /api/v1/auth/callback: OAuth callback endpoint that exchanges an authorization code for a user record.
glossary.POST /api/v1/keys: Authenticated endpoint to create a new API key scoped to a workspace.
glossary.POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh: Authenticated endpoint to generate a new secret for an existing API key.
glossary.POST /api/v1/users: Admin endpoint to create a new user record.
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces: Admin endpoint to create a new workspace.
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive: Admin endpoint to archive a workspace.
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members: Admin endpoint to add or update a workspace membership.
glossary.POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/reactivate: Admin endpoint to reactivate an archived workspace.
glossary.PUT /api/v1/users/:id: Endpoint to update a user's full_name or status; only admins can change status.
glossary.active: A user `status` value indicating the user is permitted to authenticate and use the system.
glossary.admin_tokens: Database table storing SHA-256 hashes of admin bearer tokens.
glossary.af-hub: The hub server application being built by this spec family.
glossary.af_<key_id>_<secret>: API key token format: a composite string containing a key identifier and a secret, separated by underscores, generated at key creation.
Token format prefix indicating an admin token; the full token is hashed with SHA-256 and compared against the `admin_tokens` table.
glossary.api_keys: Database table storing API key records including key_id, key_hash, workspace_id, user_id, role, revoked_at, and expires_at.
glossary.archived: A workspace state where the workspace is inactive; it must be archived before it can be deleted.
glossary.authorize_url: The OAuth provider's authorization endpoint URL, used to redirect users for login.
glossary.blocked: A user `status` value that prevents authentication even with valid tokens; checked at middleware level on every request.
glossary.config.toml: The application configuration file where OAuth providers, admin tokens, and other settings are defined.
glossary.docs/api.md: The generated REST API documentation file shipped with the af-hub repository.
glossary.expires: The expiry duration in days requested at API key creation (0, 30, 60, or 90); 0 means no expiry.
glossary.expires_at: Timestamp column in `api_keys`; when set and in the past, the key is expired and cannot authenticate.
glossary.full_name: An optional display name field on user records that any authenticated user may update for themselves.
glossary.include_archived: Query parameter on workspace list endpoint; when `true`, includes archived workspaces in the response.
glossary.key_hash: The SHA-256 hash of an API key's secret portion, stored in the `api_keys` table for comparison during authentication.
glossary.key_id: The unique identifier portion of an API key token, used to look up the key record in the `api_keys` table.
glossary.label: A human-readable name for an API key, stored for identification purposes.
glossary.membership: A record associating a user with a workspace and assigning them a role.
glossary.provider: An OAuth identity provider (e.g. GitHub) configured in the system registry.
glossary.provider_id: The unique user identifier returned by an OAuth provider, used together with `provider` to match user records.
glossary.provider_registry: The pluggable registry mapping provider names to their configuration, URLs, and field mappings.
glossary.redirect_uri: The OAuth callback URI sent to the provider during the authorization code exchange.
glossary.revoked_at: Timestamp column in `api_keys`; when set, the key is permanently revoked and cannot authenticate.
glossary.role: A per-workspace authorization level assigned to a user: `admin`, `editor`, or `reader`.
glossary.slug: A workspace identifier consisting of lowercase alphanumeric characters and hyphens, 3–64 characters, starting with a letter and not ending with a hyphen.
glossary.status: A field on user records indicating whether the user is `active` or `blocked`.
glossary.token_url: The OAuth provider's token exchange endpoint URL, used to exchange an authorization code for an access token.
glossary.upsert: A database operation that creates a record if it does not exist or updates it if it does, matched by a unique key.
glossary.userinfo_url: The OAuth provider's user info endpoint URL, used to retrieve authenticated user details.
glossary.workspace_id: The unique identifier of a workspace record, used to scope API keys and memberships.
glossary.(provider, provider_id): Composite unique constraint on user records.
glossary./api/v1/auth/*: Public auth endpoint path prefix, exempt from auth middleware.
glossary./api/v1/*: Protected API endpoint path prefix requiring Bearer token authentication.
glossary.Authorization: HTTP header carrying the Bearer token for authentication.
glossary.include_archived=true: Query parameter to include archived workspaces in list results.
glossary.user_id: Foreign key referencing a user record.
glossary.24h \u00d7 expires: Expiry calculation formula: 24 hours multiplied by the expires value in days.

user_story.goal: configure OAuth providers in config.toml and have the system use them for authentication
user_story.benefit: so that I can plug in additional identity providers without changing auth middleware or handlers

acceptance_criteria.action: defines a common provider interface with three methods: authorize URL construction, authorization code exchange for tokens, and user info extraction
ships GitHub as the default provider with built-in values for `authorize_url`, `token_url`, and `userinfo_url`; overrides from `config.toml` take precedence
allows new providers to be registered with their URLs and field mappings without modifying auth middleware or handlers
retains existing user records and `api_keys` for that provider; those users cannot re-authenticate via OAuth until linked to another provider
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a provider is removed from `config.toml`

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope describing the unsupported provider
edge_cases.error_condition: an unknown provider name is referenced during an OAuth callback

edge_cases.action: aborts the OAuth exchange, cleans up no partial state (no user record written), and returns HTTP 500 with error envelope
edge_cases.error_condition: the external identity provider's `token_url` or `userinfo_url` does not respond within a configurable timeout

user_story.goal: list available OAuth providers and exchange an authorization code for a user record
user_story.benefit: so that users can authenticate via OAuth and be represented in the system

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an array of configured provider objects containing name and `authorize_url`, with no secrets or credentials exposed
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET request is received on `GET /api/v1/auth/providers`

acceptance_criteria.action: exchanges the code with the identity provider via `token_url`, retrieves user info via `userinfo_url`, and upserts the user by `(provider, provider_id)`: creates with `status` active if new, updates `full_name` and email if existing
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST request is received on `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` with valid provider, code, and `redirect_uri`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the user object on OAuth callback success but does NOT change the user's `status` to `active`
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200, user object with status: blocked

edge_cases.error_condition: the POST body to `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` is missing required fields (provider, code, or `redirect_uri`)

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope describing the exchange failure
edge_cases.error_condition: the identity provider rejects the authorization code during exchange (e.g. expired or already used code)
the request body to `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` exceeds the body size limit

user_story.goal: have every protected endpoint validate my bearer token before processing my request
user_story.benefit: so that unauthenticated or unauthorized requests are always rejected

acceptance_criteria.action: applies to all `/api/v1/*` routes except `/api/v1/auth/*` and health probe routes
hashes the token with SHA-256, compares it against the `admin_tokens` table, and on match populates the request context with user ID, `status` active, and authentication method admin
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a request arrives with an `Authorization` header bearing a token prefixed `af_admin_...`

acceptance_criteria.action: extracts `key_id`, looks up the record in `api_keys`, hashes the secret portion with SHA-256, compares against `key_hash`, and on match populates the request context with user ID, user `status`, authentication method api_key, `workspace_id`, and `role`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a request arrives with an `Authorization` header bearing a token matching format `af_<key_id>_<secret>`

acceptance_criteria.action: rejects the request with HTTP 403 and error envelope regardless of token validity
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a valid token is verified and the user's `status` is `blocked`

edge_cases.error_condition: the `Authorization` header is missing or the token format is not recognized
an admin token's SHA-256 hash does not match any record in `admin_tokens`

edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 401, {"error": {"code": "401", "message": "invalid, revoked, or expired API key"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: an API key's `key_id` is not found in `api_keys`, its `key_hash` does not match, its `revoked_at` is set, or its `expires_at` is in the past

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 500 with error envelope; does not leak internal error details to the caller
edge_cases.error_condition: a database error occurs during token lookup

user_story.goal: have each endpoint enforce the correct role permissions before executing any business logic
user_story.benefit: so that editors and readers cannot perform admin-only operations and workspace isolation is maintained

acceptance_criteria.action: enforces three roles: admin (global full access), editor (read/write within assigned workspace), and reader (read-only within assigned workspace)
rejects the request with HTTP 403 and error envelope before the handler executes
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a non-admin user attempts to access an endpoint listed as admin-only in the permission matrix

acceptance_criteria.action: permits the update of `full_name` only; rejects any attempt to change `status` with HTTP 403
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200 for full_name update; HTTP 403 for status change attempt
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a PUT request is received on `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` where :id matches the authenticated user's own ID
acceptance_criteria.condition: the authenticated user is not an admin

acceptance_criteria.action: permits admin-authenticated requests to access all endpoints across all workspaces regardless of workspace scoping
permits editor keys and rejects reader keys with HTTP 403
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 403 for reader, pass-through for editor
acceptance_criteria.trigger: an editor or reader API key is used on a write endpoint (POST, DELETE) that requires editor or higher

edge_cases.action: treats it as an authorization failure and returns HTTP 403 with error envelope
edge_cases.error_condition: a `role` value in the request context is not one of admin, editor, reader

user_story.goal: create, list, retrieve, and update user records via REST API
user_story.benefit: so that I can manage the user population without direct database access

acceptance_criteria.action: creates a new user record with `status` active and returns the created user object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST request is received on `POST /api/v1/users` with valid username, email, `provider`, and `provider_id`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns an array of all user records
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET request is received on `GET /api/v1/users`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns the user object including workspace memberships and roles
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200, user object with memberships array
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET request is received on `GET /api/v1/users/:id` for an existing user

acceptance_criteria.action: updates `full_name` and/or `status` (active or `blocked`) on the user record and returns the updated user object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a PUT request is received on `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` with admin credentials

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 409 with error envelope describing the conflict
edge_cases.error_condition: a POST to `POST /api/v1/users` supplies a username or `(provider, provider_id)` pair that already exists
a GET or PUT request references a user ID that does not exist
a POST to `POST /api/v1/users` is missing required fields (username, email, `provider`, or `provider_id`)

user_story.goal: create, list, archive, reactivate, and delete workspaces via REST API
user_story.benefit: so that I can manage workspace lifecycle without direct database access

acceptance_criteria.action: validates `slug` format (lowercase alphanumeric and hyphens, 3–64 characters, starts with letter, does not end with hyphen) and URL format (http or https scheme with host), creates the workspace, and returns the workspace object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST request is received on `POST /api/v1/workspaces` with valid name, `slug`, and URL

acceptance_criteria.action: returns all non-archived workspaces by default; includes `archived` workspaces when `include_archived=true` query parameter is present
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET request is received on `GET /api/v1/workspaces`

acceptance_criteria.action: marks the workspace as `archived` and returns the updated workspace object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST request is received on `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive` for an active workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: marks the workspace as active and returns the updated workspace object
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST request is received on `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/reactivate` for an `archived` workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: deletes the workspace record and cascades deletion to all memberships and `api_keys` scoped to that `workspace_id`; returns success confirmation
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a DELETE request is received on `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id` for an `archived` workspace

acceptance_criteria.action: adds or updates the membership record associating the user to the workspace with the given `role`
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST request is received on `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members` with valid `user_id` and `role`

acceptance_criteria.action: returns all member records for the workspace including user IDs and roles
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET request is received on `GET /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members`

edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 409, {"error": {"code": "409", "message": "workspace name or slug already exists"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: a POST to `POST /api/v1/workspaces` supplies a name or `slug` that already exists

edge_cases.action: returns HTTP 400 with error envelope describing the validation failure
edge_cases.error_condition: a POST to `POST /api/v1/workspaces` supplies an invalid `slug` (wrong format) or invalid URL (missing scheme or non-http/https scheme)
a POST to `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive` targets a workspace that is already `archived`
a POST to `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/reactivate` targets a workspace that is not `archived`

edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 400, {"error": {"code": "400", "message": "workspace must be archived before deletion"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: a DELETE to `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id` targets a workspace that is not `archived`
any workspace endpoint references a workspace ID that does not exist
a POST to `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members` references a `user_id` that does not exist

edge_cases.action: rolls back the entire deletion transaction; workspace, memberships, and `api_keys` remain unchanged; returns HTTP 500 with error envelope
edge_cases.error_condition: the cascade deletion during `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id` fails partway through (e.g. database error after memberships deleted but before `api_keys` deleted)

user_story.goal: create, list, refresh, and revoke API keys scoped to workspaces
user_story.benefit: so that I can issue programmatic credentials with controlled expiry and scope

acceptance_criteria.action: verifies the user is a member of the workspace, generates a key in format `af_<key_id>_<secret>`, stores the SHA-256 hash of the secret as `key_hash` with `expires_at` set to `24h × expires` from creation (null if `expires` is 0), inherits the user's `role` in the workspace, and returns the full plaintext key exactly once
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST request is received on `POST /api/v1/keys` with valid `workspace_id`, `label`, and `expires` (0, 30, 60, or 90)

acceptance_criteria.action: returns all API keys across all users including expired keys
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200, array of API key objects (no plaintext secrets)
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET request is received on `GET /api/v1/keys` with an admin token

acceptance_criteria.action: returns only the authenticated user's own API keys including expired keys
acceptance_criteria.return_contract: HTTP 200, array of API key objects for the authenticated user (no plaintext secrets)
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a GET request is received on `GET /api/v1/keys` with an API key token

acceptance_criteria.action: generates a new secret for the existing `key_id`, updates `key_hash` in `api_keys`, and returns the new full key once
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a POST request is received on `POST /api/v1/keys/:key_id/refresh` for a valid key the user owns

acceptance_criteria.action: sets `revoked_at` on the `api_keys` record to the current timestamp, permanently revoking the key
acceptance_criteria.trigger: a DELETE request is received on `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id` for a key the user owns

edge_cases.return_contract: HTTP 403, {"error": {"code": "403", "message": "not a member of this workspace"}}
edge_cases.error_condition: a POST to `POST /api/v1/keys` references a `workspace_id` for which the authenticated user has no membership
a POST to `POST /api/v1/keys` supplies an `expires` value other than 0, 30, 60, or 90
a POST to `POST /api/v1/keys` references a `workspace_id` that does not exist
a POST or DELETE on a key endpoint references a `key_id` that does not exist or belongs to a different user
a reader-role user attempts to create or revoke a key via `POST /api/v1/keys` or `DELETE /api/v1/keys/:key_id`

user_story.goal: receive consistently structured error responses from every endpoint
user_story.benefit: so that my client code can handle errors uniformly without parsing varied formats

acceptance_criteria.action: formats all API error responses as {"error": {"code": "<HTTP_STATUS>", "message": "<human-readable description>"}} with the correct HTTP status code
maps status codes to meanings: 400 bad request, 401 unauthorized, 403 forbidden, 404 not found, 409 conflict, 413 payload too large, 500 internal server error
never exposes internal error details, stack traces, or database error messages in the response body for 500-level errors

user_story.goal: find complete REST API documentation in docs/api.md
user_story.benefit: so that I can integrate with af-hub without reading source code

acceptance_criteria.action: ships `docs/api.md` containing every endpoint with method, path, auth requirements, request and response body JSON examples, status codes, and error format
updates README.md to include a link to `docs/api.md`

correctness_properties.title: Admin tokens never expose plaintext in responses
correctness_properties.invariant: the system only compares SHA-256 hashes of tokens against `admin_tokens`; plaintext token values are never stored or logged

correctness_properties.title: API key secrets returned exactly once
correctness_properties.for_any: API key creation or refresh operation
correctness_properties.invariant: the plaintext `af_<key_id>_<secret>` value is included in the response exactly once and never retrievable again; only `key_hash` persists in `api_keys`

correctness_properties.title: Blocked users cannot authenticate via any token type
correctness_properties.invariant: the auth middleware always returns HTTP 403 regardless of token type (admin or API key) and regardless of token validity

correctness_properties.title: Workspace deletion is atomic
correctness_properties.invariant: either all cascade deletions (memberships and `api_keys`) succeed together with the workspace record, or none are committed; there is no partial state

correctness_properties.title: OAuth callback never re-activates blocked users
correctness_properties.for_any: OAuth callback for a user whose `status` is `blocked`
correctness_properties.invariant: the `upsert` operation on the user record never changes `status` from `blocked` to `active`

correctness_properties.title: Slug uniqueness enforced on workspace creation
correctness_properties.invariant: both name and `slug` must be unique across all workspaces; a conflict on either field causes HTTP 409 and no record is created

correctness_properties.title: Error responses never leak internal details
correctness_properties.invariant: the response body contains only the standard error envelope with message 'internal server error'; no stack traces, query text, or database error strings are included

steps.action: sends GET to `GET /api/v1/auth/providers` to discover available providers
returns array of provider names and `authorize_url` values
redirects user to GitHub `authorize_url`; receives authorization code in callback
sends POST to `POST /api/v1/auth/callback` with provider=github, code, and `redirect_uri`
exchanges code with GitHub `token_url` via HTTP client
retrieves user info from GitHub `userinfo_url`
upserts user by `(provider, provider_id)`: creates new record with `status` active
returns HTTP 200 with user object to client

execution_paths.title: Admin creates workspace and assigns member

steps.action: sends POST to `POST /api/v1/workspaces` with name, `slug`, and URL, bearing admin bearer token
hashes token with SHA-256, matches against `admin_tokens` table, populates request context with admin role
confirms admin role; permits the request
validates `slug` format and URL format, creates workspace record, returns HTTP 201 with workspace object
sends POST to `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/members` with `user_id` and `role`
re-validates admin token from `Authorization` header
upserts membership record for the user in the workspace with given `role`, returns HTTP 200 with membership object

execution_paths.title: Editor creates API key and uses it

steps.action: sends POST to `POST /api/v1/keys` with `workspace_id`, `label`, and `expires`=30, bearing editor API key token

parses `af_<key_id>_<secret>` token, looks up `key_id` in `api_keys`, verifies `key_hash` matches SHA-256 of secret, confirms `expires_at` and `revoked_at` are valid, populates context with `workspace_id`, `role` editor
confirms editor role is permitted to create keys
verifies user membership in target `workspace_id`, generates new `af_<key_id>_<secret>` token, stores `key_hash`, sets `expires_at` to 30 × 24h from now
returns HTTP 201 with plaintext key (only time it is returned)
sends subsequent request to a protected endpoint using the new key in `Authorization` header
validates new key, confirms user `status` is `active`, populates context
processes request and returns response

execution_paths.title: Blocked user is rejected at middleware

steps.action: sends request to any protected endpoint with a valid API key token
validates `key_id` and `key_hash` against `api_keys` table, finds the key valid
looks up the user's `status`; finds it is `blocked`
returns HTTP 403 with error envelope {"error": {"code": "403", "message": "user is blocked"}}; handler is never invoked

execution_paths.title: Admin archives and then deletes a workspace (full end-to-end)

steps.action: sends POST to `POST /api/v1/workspaces/:id/archive` with admin token in `Authorization` header
hashes admin token, matches `admin_tokens` table, populates context
confirms admin role; permits archive operation
marks workspace as `archived`, returns HTTP 200 with updated workspace object
sends DELETE to `DELETE /api/v1/workspaces/:id` with admin token
re-validates admin token
confirms admin role; permits delete operation
opens database transaction, deletes memberships, deletes `api_keys` scoped to `workspace_id`, deletes workspace record, commits transaction
returns HTTP 200 with {"message": "workspace deleted"}
sends PUT to `PUT /api/v1/users/:id` (own ID) with `full_name` field, bearing reader API key token
validates API key token, confirms user `status` active, populates context with user ID
detects the :id matches the authenticated user's own ID; permits `full_name` update only
updates `full_name` on the user record, returns HTTP 200 with updated user object

error_handling.condition: Authorization header is missing or token format is unrecognized
error_handling.behavior: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with standard error envelope; request processing stops

error_handling.condition: Admin token SHA-256 hash does not match any record in admin_tokens table
error_handling.behavior: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with standard error envelope

error_handling.condition: API key is not found, hash mismatch, revoked_at is set, or expires_at is in the past
error_handling.behavior: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with standard error envelope

error_handling.condition: Valid token but user status is blocked
error_handling.behavior: Auth middleware returns HTTP 403 with standard error envelope; handler is not invoked

error_handling.condition: Authenticated user's role is insufficient for the requested endpoint
error_handling.behavior: RBAC layer returns HTTP 403 with standard error envelope before handler executes

error_handling.condition: POST /api/v1/users supplies a duplicate username or (provider, provider_id) pair
error_handling.behavior: User handler returns HTTP 409 with standard error envelope; no user record is created

error_handling.condition: GET or PUT on /api/v1/users/:id references a non-existent user ID
error_handling.behavior: User handler returns HTTP 404 with standard error envelope

error_handling.condition: POST /api/v1/workspaces supplies a duplicate name or slug
error_handling.behavior: Workspace handler returns HTTP 409 with standard error envelope; no workspace is created

error_handling.condition: POST /api/v1/workspaces supplies invalid slug format or non-http/https URL
error_handling.behavior: Workspace handler returns HTTP 400 with standard error envelope; no workspace is created

error_handling.condition: Archive requested on an already-archived workspace
error_handling.behavior: Workspace handler returns HTTP 400 with standard error envelope

error_handling.condition: Reactivate requested on a workspace that is not archived
error_handling.behavior: Workspace handler returns HTTP 400 with standard error envelope

error_handling.condition: Delete requested on a workspace that is not archived
error_handling.behavior: Workspace handler returns HTTP 400 with standard error envelope; workspace is not deleted

error_handling.condition: Any workspace endpoint references a workspace ID that does not exist
error_handling.behavior: Workspace handler returns HTTP 404 with standard error envelope

error_handling.condition: Cascade deletion transaction fails partway through workspace deletion
error_handling.behavior: Entire transaction is rolled back; workspace, memberships, and api_keys remain unchanged; HTTP 500 returned

error_handling.condition: POST /api/v1/keys user is not a member of the referenced workspace_id
error_handling.behavior: API key handler returns HTTP 403 with standard error envelope; no key is created

error_handling.condition: POST /api/v1/keys supplies an expires value other than 0, 30, 60, or 90
error_handling.behavior: API key handler returns HTTP 400 with standard error envelope; no key is created

error_handling.condition: Key endpoint references a key_id that does not exist or belongs to another user
error_handling.behavior: API key handler returns HTTP 404 with standard error envelope

error_handling.condition: OAuth callback request body is missing provider, code, or redirect_uri fields
error_handling.behavior: Auth handler returns HTTP 400 with standard error envelope

error_handling.condition: Identity provider rejects the authorization code during token exchange
error_handling.behavior: Auth handler returns HTTP 400 with standard error envelope; no user record is written

error_handling.condition: Identity provider token_url or userinfo_url does not respond within configured timeout
error_handling.behavior: Auth handler aborts the exchange, writes no user record, and returns HTTP 500 with standard error envelope

error_handling.condition: Database error occurs during any token lookup in auth middleware
error_handling.behavior: Auth middleware returns HTTP 500 with standard error envelope without leaking internal details

error_handling.condition: Request body exceeds the configured body size limit on any endpoint
error_handling.behavior: Handler returns HTTP 413 with standard error envelope

symbols.name: Echo
symbols.notes: Main Echo server instance; used to register middleware and route groups

symbols.name: Group
symbols.notes: Creates a route group; used to apply auth middleware to /api/v1/* while excluding /api/v1/auth/*

symbols.name: MiddlewareFunc
symbols.notes: Middleware function type for auth and RBAC middleware

symbols.name: Context
symbols.notes: Request context interface; used to store authenticated user ID, role, workspace_id, and auth method

symbols.name: HandlerFunc
symbols.notes: Handler function type for all route handlers
